author: @n0tfunnyHaHa
text:
"Thank God" in Arabic is most commonly expressed as Alhamdulillah (الحمد لله), which translates to "praise be to God" or "all praise and thanks be to Allah". It is used by both Muslims and Arabic-speaking Christians to express gratitude for blessings, relief in difficult situations, or when answering44 questions about well-being. 
Wikipedia
Wikipedia
 +6
Key Details and Variations:
Pronunciation: Al-ham-du-lillah.
Meaning: It combines Al (the), hamd (praise/gratitude), and li (for/to) + Allah (God).
Usage: It is used daily to express thanks for health, safety, or to show gratitude during trials.
Context: It is often used as a response to "How are you?" to mean "I am fine, praise God".
Alternative Phrases: Sometimes Ash-shukru lillah (الشكر لله) is used, which means "thanks to God".
